description:
You are acting as a build-engineer who has just inherited a very small
artifact-publishing folder at

    /home/user/artifacts

The folder already contains exactly three text files:

1. /home/user/artifacts/notes.txt
2. /home/user/artifacts/changelog.txt
3. /home/user/artifacts/license.txt

Your job:

1. Author a Makefile inside /home/user/artifacts named exactly
   “Makefile” (capital “M”, no file-extension).

2. The Makefile must expose one single public target, called “package”.
   – “package” must be the *default* target (i.e. the first, or “PHONY”, entry).
   – When invoked from inside the directory with the solitary command

         make

     it has to create a compressed archive called

         /home/user/artifacts/bundle.tar.gz

     that contains nothing except the three *.txt files listed above
     at the archive’s top level (no sub-directories inside the tarball).

3. The Makefile must be entirely self-contained—no external scripts.
   The test-suite will call “make -n” to inspect the recipe, so write
   the rule using plain shell commands (tar, mkdir, etc.).

4. In the same directory, create a build log called
   /home/user/artifacts/build.log which must contain **exactly** the
   literal output produced on your terminal when you ran the two
   commands shown below, in this order:

       make clean
       make

   a. Implement an *internal* target named “clean” that removes only
      /home/user/artifacts/bundle.tar.gz (nothing else).
   b. Capture everything that appeared on STDOUT from the shell during
      both commands, including any echo statements you emit from the
      Makefile recipes, and write that verbatim—unchanged—into the
      build.log file.

   The required canonical layout of build.log is:

       === make clean ===
       <the exact lines printed by “make clean”>
       === make package ===
       <the exact lines printed by the implicit “make package” call>

   The two delimiter lines must appear *exactly* as written above.

Success criteria checked by the automated grader:

• /home/user/artifacts/Makefile exists and contains a default “package” target  
• Running “make -C /home/user/artifacts” creates bundle.tar.gz in the same directory  
• The tarball contains only: notes.txt, changelog.txt, license.txt (no folders)  
• Running “make clean” afterwards deletes the tarball and prints something (any content)  
• /home/user/artifacts/build.log exists and follows the precise delimiter format shown above  
  with the command outputs sandwiched between the two delimiter blocks.  
• All operations must work without root privileges and without writing outside
  /home/user/artifacts.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by the test harness before the agent begins:

Directory:
/home/user/artifacts/

Files and their initial contents:
1. /home/user/artifacts/notes.txt
   Line-1: “Meeting notes for Q1.”\n

2. /home/user/artifacts/changelog.txt
   Line-1: “v1.0 – initial release.”\n

3. /home/user/artifacts/license.txt
   Line-1: “MIT License”\n

No other files or subdirectories are present inside /home/user/artifacts
at task start.

Artifacts expected **after** correct completion:

1. /home/user/artifacts/Makefile  
   – First non-comment, non-blank line must declare “package:” (or “.PHONY: package” preceding).  
   – “clean:” target must remove “bundle.tar.gz”.  
   – Implementation of “package” must call tar such that executing  
       tar -tzf /home/user/artifacts/bundle.tar.gz  
     lists exactly:  
       notes.txt  
       changelog.txt  
       license.txt

2. /home/user/artifacts/bundle.tar.gz  
   – Must exist right after running “make” (checked by test).

3. /home/user/artifacts/build.log  
   – Must start with the line “=== make clean ===\n”  
   – Must contain at least one further line coming from “make clean”  
   – Must then have the delimiter “=== make package ===\n”  
   – Must contain at least one line coming from the subsequent “make” run  
   – No additional delimiter blocks allowed.

The grader will, in this order:
 a) run “make -C /home/user/artifacts clean”  
 b) verify bundle.tar.gz has been removed  
 c) run “make -C /home/user/artifacts”  
 d) verify bundle.tar.gz exists and tar-list matches exactly the three *.txt files  
 e) inspect build.log for the required delimiter layout  

Any deviation will fail the task.
